content.full_text:
RAvI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR PoLITIcAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
April 22, 1997
John Bovée
The Bovée Company
1127 11th Street, Suite 226
Sacramento, CA 95814
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 1-97-075
Dear Mr. Bovée:
This letter is a response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
Since your advice request is not on behalf of a specific
official, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.
2
QUESTIONS & CONCLUSIONS
1. Under section 85305, how does a member ofthe State Legislature who isfundraising
to eliminate a campaign deficit that occurred before Januaiy 1, 1997, handle thefundraising
expenses that occur as a result oft?ying to eliminate that deficit? What about the use of
professionalfundraisers?
Proposition 208 went into effect on January 1, 1997. Among other things, Proposition
208 prohibits candidates from accepting campaign contributions during the period the off-year
ban is in effect. (Section 85305.) However, candidates may accept contributions during the off-
year period to retire a campaign debt incurred prior to the effective date ofProposition 208.
(Section 85305(c); Emergency Regulation 18530.7(b)(1).) You would like to know how a
candidate may pay for the fundraising expenses that occur as a result of trying to eliminate the
pre-1997 campaign debt.
Emergency regulation 1 8530.7(b)(1)
3 provides that funds may be collected at any time to
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 infommi assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329.)
‘ Emergency regulations expire by operation of law 120 days after adoption. Regulation 18530.7 will
expire on May 7, 1997. The Commission may adopt the regulation as a permanent regulation before the expiration
date.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. 1-97-075
Page 2
retire a campaign debt incurred prior to January 1, 1997. We construe this provision to allow
candidates to use a portion of the contributions, which were raised during the off-year ban to
retire a pre-1997 campaign debt, to pay for the reasonable expenses necessarily incurred as a
result of collecting such contributions. Such expenses may include the cost ofhiring a
professional fundraiser. To illustrate, if a candidate spends $100 to raise contributions totaling
$1,000, $100 out of the $1,000 may go towards the amount the candidate had to spend to collect
the contributions.
2. Do expenses resultingfrom retiring a pre-1997 campaign debt incurred with respect
to the 1998 election, including the expenses ofhiring aprofessionalfundraiser, count against the
1998 campaign contribution and spending limits. Does it matter ftheprofessionalfundraiser is
paid on commission (15 percent ofthe net raised) or straight salary ($3,000 a month)for deficit
reductionfundraising assistance?
Under Proposition 208, the contribution limit applicable to legislative districts is $250 per
person per election. (Section 85301(b).) If a legislative candidate agrees to accept the voluntary
spending limit, the applicable contribution limit for that candidate is $500 per person per
election. (Section 85402(b).) For Assembly candidates, the voluntary spending limit is
$150,000 in the primary election and $200,000 in the general election. (Section 85400.) For
Senate candidates, the voluntary spending limit is $300,000 in the primary election and $400,000
in the general election. (Id.) Proposition 208’s contribution limits and voluntary expenditures
ceilings apply to all legislative elections held after January 1, 1997.
Funds raised to pay the expenses resulting from retiring a pre-1997 campaign debt
incurred with respect to the 1998 election, including the expenses of hiring a professional
fundraiser, are in effect funds raised for the 1998 election. As such, these funds count against the
contribution and spending limits applicable to the 1998 election. The result is the same whether
the professional fundraiser is paid on commission or straight salary.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
Br1lia Butcher
Graduate Assistant, Legal Division
SGC:JB:ak
